message.content:
<deliberation>
Plan:
- Map main_entry_nodes to provided regulation texts and identify cross-references.
- Evaluate policy chunk against combined requirements (including referenced nodes).
- Only flag deviations if there is a concrete contradiction/narrowing/etc. supported by direct GDPR/AI Act quotes; treat omissions as Non-Deviation per instruction 4.
- REG-091 text is not provided; per instruction 4.1, classify as Non-Deviation.

Analysis by node:
1) REG-036 (Art14(4)):
- Cross-references: Art14(1) (REG-033), Art14(2) (REG-034), Art14(3) (REG-035) because 14(4) is “for the purpose of implementing paragraphs 1–3”.
- Art14(4) requires enabling oversight persons to understand capacities/limitations, monitor, be aware of automation bias, interpret outputs, decide to disregard/override, and intervene/stop.
- Art14(3)(b) explicitly allows measures identified by provider but implemented by deployer; thus provider guidance to a third party/deployer is contemplated.
- Policy: Requires designing system elements “when possible”; otherwise provide guidance to the third party; define/document evaluation methods and metrics for oversight/control; users conduct evaluations on near-release builds; if criteria unmet, consult and develop a gap management plan.
- No explicit contradiction: policy’s “when not responsible, provide guidance” aligns with Art14(3)(b). No statements that negate the need to enable override/stop/etc. Omissions (e.g., no explicit “stop button” text) are to be treated as Non-Deviation.

2) REG-045 (Art26(3)):
- Cross-references: Art26(1) (REG-043) and Art26(2) (REG-044) because 26(3) refers to “obligations set out in paragraphs 1 and 2”.
- Art26(3) preserves deployers’ freedom to organize resources/activities to implement oversight measures indicated by the provider and is without prejudice to other obligations.
- Policy: Internal process requirements (define/document methods, metrics, responsible release criteria; users conduct near-release evaluations; consult reviewers/ORA for sensitive uses; create a plan if gaps remain). No statement limiting a deployer’s freedom to organize resources; rather, it describes internal governance.
- Therefore, no contradiction or narrowing.

3) REG-091:
- No regulatory text provided. Per instruction 4.1, without a quote, must classify as Non-Deviation.

Conclusion: No deviations for the three main nodes; include considered references for REG-036 and REG-045; empty references for REG-091.
</deliberation>
